commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=b33e898127600ef36d95e128b4d7afc616b31bdb)
commands.review_summary:
The PR advertises tart desktop capability, enables guest macOS Screen Sharing during desktop acquire, adds tart provider tests, and updates tart provider docs.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review finding: source inspection shows the new tart `FeatureDesktop` bit would route `crabbox webvnc` into `directSSHWebVNC`, whose remote command requires target-side noVNC/websockify and `ss`. I did not run a live tart host.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The contributor needs to repair the tart WebVNC bridge path and add real Apple Silicon/tart proof before this can be merge-ready.

target.title: feat(tart): desktop/VNC support via guest Screen Sharing
target.body:
## Summary

Follow-up to the tart provider (#209), which shipped headless and explicitly deferred desktop/VNC. This adds visible-desktop support so `crabbox warmup --provider tart --desktop` + `crabbox webvnc` works, matching the other desktop-capable providers.

## What changed

- **`FeatureDesktop`** is now advertised in the tart provider spec, so `--desktop` is accepted instead of rejected.
- During Acquire, for `--desktop` leases the provider enables the guest's built-in **macOS Screen Sharing** over `tart exec`:
  - generates a 16-char VNC password at `/var/db/crabbox/vnc.password`,
  - sets the lease user's account password to match (`dscl . -passwd`),
  - starts `com.apple.screensharing`.
- The VM still launches `--no-graphics`; Screen Sharing is a network VNC service on `127.0.0.1:5900` and doesn't need the local display.

This is the same managed-VNC contract the cloud/host providers use (`waitForLoopbackVNC` on `127.0.0.1:5900`, password via `/var/db/crabbox/vnc.password`), so `crabbox webvnc` tunnels it over SSH with no further wiring. macOS leases are already treated as screen-sharing-capable by the capability layer, so no label plumbing was needed beyond the standard `directLeaseLabels` desktop flag.

## How it works

```
crabbox warmup --provider tart --desktop
crabbox webvnc --id <lease-id>
```

Acquire: `tart clone` → configure → `tart run --no-graphics` → `tart ip` → inject SSH key → **(desktop) enable Screen Sharing via `tart exec`** → wait for SSH readiness. `webvnc` then probes `nc -z 127.0.0.1 5900` over SSH, reads the password with `sudo cat /var/db/crabbox/vnc.password`, and bridges to the portal.

## Files

| File | What |
|------|------|
| `internal/providers/tart/provider.go` | advertise `FeatureDesktop` |
| `internal/providers/tart/backend.go` | `enableScreenSharing` + Acquire hook (gated on `--desktop`) |
| `internal/providers/tart/provider_test.go` | tests: spec feature, screen-sharing script, bad-user rejection |
| `docs/providers/tart.md` | Desktop/VNC section; remove the "not supported" note |

## Test status

- `go test -race ./internal/providers/tart/` — green (race detector clean)
- `go vet` / build — clean

**Live proof:** unit-tested here; an end-to-end Screen-Sharing-over-`webvnc` capture requires Apple Silicon + tart and will be attached as redacted terminal output / recording on the host with tart available. The change is mechanical parity with the existing managed-VNC providers.

## Not included

- A VNC-only (no SSH) flow — desktop still rides on the SSH lease.
- Resolution/scaling controls.
target.author: anagnorisis2peripeteia
